meta.title: Historical Milestones
meta.description: Key developments in AI that move the hands toward midnight.

timeline.title: Reasoning-First Models
timeline.description: OpenAI o3 and xAI Grok 3 extend inference-time computation to improve performance on complex tasks. Both emphasize multi-step reasoning and tool integration, reflecting a shift toward test-time scaling as a primary capability lever.

timeline.title: Deliberate Reasoning Models
timeline.description: OpenAI o1-preview applies reinforcement learning to train models that spend extended time reasoning before responding. This improves accuracy on math, coding, and scientific problems where chain-of-thought depth correlates with correctness.

timeline.title: Text-to-Video Leap
timeline.description: OpenAI Sora and Google DeepMind Veo generate high-fidelity video from text prompts and image inputs. Both use diffusion-based architectures trained on large video datasets, producing temporally coherent outputs at resolutions suitable for production use.

timeline.title: Apple Intelligence Launch
timeline.description: Apple Intelligence integrates generative AI into iOS 18, iPadOS 18, and macOS Sequoia. Processing is split between on-device inference and Private Cloud Compute, keeping user data from being logged or used for model training.

timeline.title: AlphaFold 3 for Biomolecular Systems
timeline.description: AlphaFold 3 by DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs extends structure prediction beyond proteins to include DNA, RNA, and small-molecule ligands. A diffusion-based architecture replaces the earlier Evoformer pipeline, enabling joint modeling of molecular interactions.

timeline.title: Efficient Small LLMs
timeline.description: Microsoft's Phi-3-mini achieves competitive benchmark scores with 3.8 billion parameters, small enough to run locally on mobile hardware. Performance is attributed to high-quality synthetic training data rather than increased model scale.

timeline.title: Multimodal + Long-Context Frontier
timeline.description: GPT-4o, Claude 3, and Gemini 1.5 each support inputs combining text, images, and audio with context windows up to one million tokens. This enables document-level reasoning and real-time multimodal interaction within a single model call.

timeline.title: Llama 3 Open Frontier
timeline.description: Meta released Llama 3 in 8B and 70B parameter sizes under a permissive license. The models matched or exceeded several closed-source models on standard benchmarks, accelerating open-weight fine-tuning and deployment across research and commercial applications.

timeline.title: Enterprise RAG + Hybrid Models
timeline.description: Cohere Command R and AI21 Jamba target retrieval-augmented generation at production scale. Command R optimizes for grounded responses over long documents; Jamba combines Mamba state-space layers with transformer blocks to reduce memory cost at extended context lengths.

timeline.title: Blackwell AI Infrastructure
timeline.description: NVIDIA's Blackwell GPU architecture introduces a second-generation transformer engine and NVLink Switch supporting 72-GPU clusters. It targets trillion-parameter training and reduces inference cost per token compared to the prior Hopper generation.

timeline.title: Asia's Frontier LLM Wave
timeline.description: Baidu, Alibaba, Tencent, Huawei, Naver, and Samsung each released large language models optimized for regional languages and enterprise use cases. These releases signaled that frontier LLM development was no longer concentrated exclusively in US-based labs.

timeline.title: Embodied AI Models
timeline.description: Google's PaLM-E and RT-2 embed language model representations directly into robotic control pipelines. RT-2 fine-tunes a vision-language model on robot trajectory data, enabling it to interpret novel instructions and generalize to objects not seen during robot training.

timeline.title: Agentic Tool Use
timeline.description: The ReAct framework interleaves language model reasoning steps with external tool calls, enabling verifiable, multi-step task execution. AutoGPT implemented this pattern autonomously, demonstrating persistent task loops without per-step human prompting.

timeline.title: Frontier LLM Competition
timeline.description: Anthropic Claude 2, Google Gemini 1.0, and xAI Grok each extended instruction-following capability and context length beyond GPT-3.5-level baselines. Their release compressed the performance gap at the frontier and increased competitive pressure on OpenAI.

timeline.title: GPT-4 & Multimodal Reasoning
timeline.description: GPT-4 accepted image and text inputs and improved substantially on professional benchmarks including bar and medical licensing exams. Its performance gains over GPT-3.5 were attributed to scale, RLHF refinements, and expanded pretraining data diversity.

timeline.title: ChatGPT Launch
timeline.description: ChatGPT applied RLHF to GPT-3.5 to produce a conversational interface that reached one million users in five days. It demonstrated that fine-tuning for dialogue and instruction-following could make large language models practically usable without prompt engineering expertise.

timeline.title: AlphaFold2 Breakthrough
timeline.description: AlphaFold2 predicted protein 3D structures from amino acid sequences at accuracy matching experimental crystallography. It used attention-based neural networks trained on the Protein Data Bank, solving a problem that had resisted computational biology for over fifty years.

timeline.title: GPT-3 Released
timeline.description: GPT-3's 175 billion parameters enabled few-shot task completion from plain-text prompts alone, without weight updates. It established that sufficient scale in autoregressive language modeling produces emergent instruction-following behavior absent from smaller predecessors.

timeline.title: BERT: Bidirectional Language Understanding
timeline.description: BERT pre-trains transformer encoders using masked language modeling and next-sentence prediction, producing bidirectional token representations. Fine-tuned BERT set new records on eleven NLP benchmarks, including SQuAD and GLUE, and became the basis for Google Search ranking improvements.

timeline.title: Transformer Architecture
timeline.description: Vaswani et al. replaced recurrent networks with scaled dot-product attention in an encoder-decoder architecture for sequence transduction. Transformers parallelized training across sequence positions, enabling larger model scales and forming the architectural basis for all major subsequent language models.

timeline.title: AlphaGo Defeats Lee Sedol
timeline.description: AlphaGo defeated world champion Lee Sedol 4–1 using Monte Carlo tree search guided by deep neural network policy and value functions trained on human games and self-play. It demonstrated that learned heuristics could replace hand-crafted evaluation in high-dimensional combinatorial search.

timeline.title: Deep Learning Revolution
timeline.description: AlexNet's convolutional neural network reduced ImageNet top-5 error to 15.3%, compared to 26.2% for the next best entry. Trained on two GPUs with ReLU activations and dropout regularization, it demonstrated that deep networks on commodity hardware could decisively outperform hand-engineered vision pipelines.

timeline.title: Deep Blue Defeats Kasparov
timeline.description: IBM's Deep Blue defeated Garry Kasparov in a six-game match, the first time a computer had beaten a reigning world chess champion under standard tournament conditions. It used custom VLSI chips to evaluate 200 million positions per second combined with handcrafted evaluation functions.

timeline.title: ELIZA
timeline.description: ELIZA, developed by Joseph Weizenbaum at MIT, simulated conversation by pattern-matching user input and substituting it into scripted response templates. Despite having no semantic understanding, users attributed comprehension and empathy to it, prompting early discussion of human-computer interaction and anthropomorphism.

timeline.title: Dartmouth Conference
timeline.description: The Dartmouth Summer Research Project on Artificial Intelligence, organized by John McCarthy, Marvin Minsky, Nathaniel Rochester, and Claude Shannon, formally established AI as a research discipline. Attendees proposed that machine simulation of learning and intelligence was tractable and worth sustained scientific investigation.

timeline.title: The Turing Test
timeline.description: Alan Turing's paper 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' proposed replacing the question of machine consciousness with a behavioral criterion: whether a machine could conduct text-based conversation indistinguishable from a human. This framing shifted AI evaluation toward empirical, task-based criteria.
